summary: For Most Investors, Mutual Funds ही काफी हैं. In this episode of This or That Podcast, Neil Borate — one of India’s most respected personal-finance journalists and authors — joins Bhartendra Singh to decode what truly matters in investing.

chapters.label: Highlight & Introduction
What Options do People have for Investments ?
What is an Ideal way to Start Investing ?
What is the Best way to in Equity ?
“Buy and Forget” – when it actually works
How to Construct a Good Portfolio ?
Flexi Cap Vs Multicap Vs Multi Asset
What are reasonable return expectations?
Core–Satellite framework for balanced investing
Why Global Equity matters for Indians I Global exposure = insurance for your portfolio
How GIFT City opens India’s gateway to global investing
KYC for Gift City
Getting International Investing Exposure Through Multiple Option & When Should We Start?
NPS in the new tax regime – why it still works
EPF withdrawals: why claims get rejected?
Options in NPS
Suitability of Different Products
What is a Good Financial Plan ?
Health insurance
How to judge a health insurer?
How to pick a mutual fund (beyond past returns)
How to Look At Underperforming Fund ?
Direct vs regular plans: who should choose what.
Neil's Portfolio
Behaviour is more important than Product – your real edge
Crypto: taxes, volatility, unclear use case.
Closing Thoughts : One person to follow: Dhirendra Kumar.

tool.label: Asset Allocator
tool.reason: This episode is really about your mix. Put in your numbers and see the equity, debt, gold and international split that actually fits you.

disclaimer: The views expressed in this podcast are for educational and informational purposes only. They do not constitute investment advice or a recommendation to buy, sell, or hold any financial product. Please consult a SEBI-registered financial advisor before making any investment decisions.
